NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (TRANSACTION ID # 77934188) FILED BY DEFENDANT CARR, CHRISTINE HEARING SET FOR JAN-13-2026 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 301

DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANT CHRISTINE CARR'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND GOOD CAUSE SHOWN FOR ALL RELIEF SOUGHT BY THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF MUST SERVE VERIFIED CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO ALL OF THE INTERROGATORIES AND DOCUMENTS REQUESTS WITHOUT OBJECTIONS AND PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS AND PAY $1,180 IN REASONABLE MONETARY SANCTIONS NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 11, 2026. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-05-2026 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2026 Set For Trial On Jul-06-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jan-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHRISTINE CARR'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents

Defendant Christine Carr's motion to compel responses to special interrogatories and document requests and for monetary sanctions is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown for all relief sought by the motion.

Plaintiff must serve verified code-compliant responses to all of the interrogatories and documents requests without objections and produce all responsive documents and pay $1,180 in reasonable monetary sanctions no later than February 11, 2026.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
